title: Astracantha pachyacantha
taxonomy: Astracantha pachyacantha is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is commonly known as the legume family. This classification places it among other legume species such as beans, peas, and lentils. At the genus level, Astracantha pachyacantha is part of the Astracantha genus, which contains several species of thorny shrubs. The species is further categorized within the Astracantha genus due to its unique characteristics. Further classification at the species level places it as a distinct entity within the Astracantha genus.

intro: Astracantha pachyacantha, also known as Oligomeris pachyacantha, is a type of plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha pachyacantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Astracantha pachyacantha is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
